Title: Up-regulation of GhTT2-3A in cotton fibres during secondary wall thickening results in brown fibres with improved quality

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts synergistically with GhbHLH130D (TT8 homolog); Arabidopsis-like TT2–TT8 regulatory module inferred; TTG1 not directly validated in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S (constitutive, callus overexpression); FbL2A (fibre-specific, secondary wall-thickening stage)
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in developing brown fibres; preferentially elevated around secondary wall-thickening stage and at 20–22 DPA in brown fibres, low in white fibres
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression co-segregation with PA accumulation in RILs; fine mapping of Lc1; transgenic callus overexpression; fibre-specific transgenic expression in cotton; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase promoter activation assay
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of 22-DPA fibres, GenBank SRA PRJNA416219
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Natural brown fibre pigmentation; antioxidant flavonoid polymer relevant to eco-friendly naturally coloured cotton
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Constitutive 35S overexpression did not yield regenerated cotton plants, likely due to growth/regeneration inhibition by constitutive PA activation; coding region identical between brown-fibre T586 and white-fibre Yumian No. 1, implying regulatory up-regulation causes phenotype; upstream causative mutation remains unresolved
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Primary determinant of Lc1 brown fibre trait; sufficient to induce PA accumulation and brown mature fibres when up-regulated in fibres
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive activation likely inhibits regeneration/development; fibre-specific late expression avoided detectable penalties in lint percentage and major fibre quality traits
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Jimian No. 14 (J14); null segregant control; brown-fibre controls T586-derived materials and Z82
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target for naturally coloured cotton; fibre-stage-specific expression can decouple brown colour from yield/fibre-quality penalties; potential stacking with GhbHLH130D may deepen colour
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes PA biosynthesis/accumulation and confers brown fibre pigmentation without major fibre-quality penalty when expressed during secondary wall thickening
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates whole PA pathway; up-regulates PA structural genes including GhANR and GhLAR; also up-regulates GhbHLH130D
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GhbHLH130D; PA structural genes including GhANR (Gh_A05G1424) and GhLAR (Gh_D12G1686); broader PA pathway genes from PAL to ANR
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; indirect and synergistic activation of PA structural genes via induction and cooperation with GhbHLH130D
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in brown fibres, especially developing fibres around 20–22 DPA; in FbL2A transgenics induced preferentially after 16 DPA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhTT2-3A was the only TT2 homolog strongly associated with brown fibre PA accumulation and co-segregated with the Lc1 phenotype. 2. Up-regulation of GhTT2-3A activated PA pathway genes and increased PA accumulation in cotton calli and fibres. 3. Fibre-specific late expression generated brown mature fibres while maintaining lint percentage and major fibre-quality traits near white-fibre controls. 4. GhTT2-3A activated GhbHLH130D and synergized with it to strongly activate GhANR and GhLAR promoters.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fibre developmental stage, especially secondary wall-thickening stage; analysed at 11, 20, 22 and 27 DPA
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Lc1-associated cis-regulatory up-regulation inferred; exact upstream causative factor unknown

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Synergistic functional interaction with GhTT2-3A in activating PA structural gene promoters
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated in FbL2A:GhTT2-3A fibres at 22 DPA (>4.5-fold by RNA-seq)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; dual-luciferase promoter activation assay in Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of 22-DPA fibres, GenBank SRA PRJNA416219
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Natural brown pigmentation in cotton fibre via PA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Acts downstream of GhTT2-3A in a feed-forward-like regulatory module to amplify PA gene activation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper proposes co-expression with GhTT2-3A as a strategy to obtain darker brown fibres
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important co-activator that markedly boosts PA structural gene expression together with GhTT2-3A
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Jimian No. 14 transgenic fibres for RNA-seq context; N. benthamiana for transient assay
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate stacking partner with GhTT2-3A to intensify brown fibre colour
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Synergistically enhances activation of PA structural genes, thereby promoting PA accumulation/brown pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates GhANR and GhLAR promoters moderately alone and strongly with GhTT2-3A
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; synergistic co-activation with GhTT2-3A
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhbHLH130D was significantly up-regulated in GhTT2-3A-expressing fibres. 2. GhbHLH130D alone moderately activated GhANR and GhLAR promoters. 3. Co-expression with GhTT2-3A dramatically enhanced promoter activation, supporting a synergistic PA regulatory module.
